LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ELIAS NAGHI JOINDER IN THE MOTION TO TRANSFER ACTION AND FOR SANCTIONS OF DEFENDANTS DAVID NAGHI AND PANTEA NAGHI IS OFF CALENDAR PER REQUEST OF MOVING PARTY; EMAIL DATED 2/12/19. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO CSR# 8600 EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS DAVID NAGHI AND PANTEA NAGHI'S MOTION TO TRANSFER AND FOR SANCTIONS. DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO CHANGE VENUE IS GRANTED. THIS ACTION IS TRANSFERRED TO THE CENTRAL DISTRICT OF LOS ANGELES COUNTY AND PLAINTIFF IS TO PAY ALL REQUIRED TRANSFER COSTS. THE PROPER COUNTY FOR COMMENCEMENT OF A PROCEEDING CONCERNING A LIVING TRUST IS THE COUNTY WHERE THE PRINCIPAL PLACE OF ADMINISTRATION OF THE TRUST IS LOCATED. (PROB. CODE 17005(A)(1).) PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO PROVIDE A DECLARATION THAT ESTABLISHES THE PRINCIPAL PLACE OF ADMINISTRATION OF THE TRUST, DEFINED AS "THE USUAL PLACE WHERE THE DAY-TO-DAY ACTIVITY OF THE TRUST IS CARRIED ON BY THE TRUSTEE OR ITS REPRESENTATIVE WHO IS PRIMARILY RESPONSIBLE FOR THE ADMINISTRATION OF THE TRUST." (PROB. CODE 17002(A).) THE DECLARATION OF MR. SALAMA SHOWS ONLY THAT WHEN HE MEETS IN PERSON TO CONDUCT TRUST BUSINESS WITH PLAINTIFF, THOSE MEETINGS OCCUR IN SAN FRANCISCO. THE DECLARATION IS SILENT REGARDING THE FREQUENCY OF THESE MEETINGS OR WHETHER THE DAY-TO-DAY OPERATIONS OF THE TRUST ARE ALWAYS CONDUCTED IN SUCH IN PERSON MEETINGS. THE UNDISPUTED EVIDENCE SHOWS THAT PLAINTIFF TRUSTEE RESIDES IN AND WORKS IN LOS ANGELES. THE COURT REASONABLY INFERS BASED ON PLAINTIFF'S RESIDENCE AND PLACE OF WORK THAT THE TRUST IS ADMINISTERED IN LOS ANGELES. (SEE PROB. CODE 17002(B)(1) [IF THE PRINCIPAL PLACE OF ADMINISTRATION CANNOT BE DETERMINED, VENUE IS AT THE TRUSTEE'S RESIDENCE OR USUAL PLACE OF BUSINESS].) DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED BECAUSE THERE WAS A REASONABLE BASIS FOR FILING THIS ACTION IN SAN FRANCISCO. (AS FULLY REPORTED IN THE REPORTER'S TRANSCRIPT). PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S RULING AND IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: S. LE, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jun-07-2019 Off Calendar

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-04-2019 Continued To Jun-11-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

(Renewed) Motion To Transfer And For Sanctions.
Off Calendar Mar-19-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Joseph Salama, esq. (415-948-9030) for Plaintiff.
Richard D. Williams, esq. (310-982-2733) for Defendants.

RULING - A R G U E D. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendants David Naghi and Pantea Naghi's Motion To Transfer And For Sanctions.

Defendants' motion to change venue is granted. This action is transferred to the central district of Los Angeles County and plaintiff is to pay all required transfer costs. The proper county for commencement of a proceeding concerning a living trust is the county where the principal place of administration of the trust is located. (Prob. Code 17005(a)(1).) Plaintiff has failed to provide a declaration that establishes the principal place of administration of the trust, defined as "the usual place where the day-to-day activity of the trust is carried on by the trustee or its representative who is primarily responsible for the administration of the trust." (Prob. Code 17002(a).) The declaration of Mr. Salama shows only that when he meets in person to conduct trust business with plaintiff, those meetings occur in San Francisco. The declaration is silent regarding the frequency of these meetings or whether the day-to-day operations of the trust are always conducted in such in person meetings. The undisputed evidence shows that plaintiff trustee resides in and works in Los Angeles. The court reasonably infers based on plaintiff's residence and place of work that the trust is administered in Los Angeles. (See Prob. Code 17002(b)(1) [if the principal place of administration cannot be determined, venue is at the trustee's residence or usual place of business].) Defendant's request for sanctions is denied because there was a reasonable basis for filing this action in San Francisco. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript).

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
